note: Per-portal how-to-apply guides. Keyed by slug. `aliases` lets the opportunity source string match back to the right portal even when wording differs slightly. Author/edit freely, this file is loaded by bundle.py into data.js and rendered on directory.html and opportunity.html. Reviewed and revised 27 May 2026 against current public sources (Procurement Act 2023 / Central Digital Platform, recent SBS framework, NOE CPC Atamis migration, etc).

fts.name: Find a Tender Service (FTS)
fts.intro: Find a Tender is the UK's central public procurement notice platform for above-threshold opportunities and Procurement Act 2023 notices. Under the Act it has become part of the Central Digital Platform, where suppliers can store commonly-used information once and reuse it across procurements. The tender response itself usually still happens on the buyer's named e-sourcing system (Atamis, Jaggaer, SAP Ariba, ProContract, In-tend, Delta, PCS-Tender, etc).
hoops: No registration needed to browse or set up keyword alerts.
When a notice catches your eye, follow it through to the buyer's named e-sourcing system. That is where you actually register and submit.

Have a basic supplier pack ready before you start: company info, two or three reference clients, insurance certificates. For clinical or data products, expect to need security/clinical assurance evidence (ISO 27001 or a roadmap, Cyber Essentials, DCB0129 where the product affects clinical decisions, DPIA/DTAC or equivalent). Exact requirements vary by framework and lot.
steps: Create a saved search with CPV codes for digital health (72000000 IT services, 85100000 health services, 33000000 medical devices) and the keywords you care about.
Read the notice in full: contract value, lot structure, evaluation criteria, deadlines, and any pre-qualification requirements.
Follow the 'How to access this opportunity' link to register on the buyer's e-sourcing portal.
Download the SQ and ITT documents as soon as they are available. Windows are tight.
fts.gotchas: FTS only carries notices above the relevant public procurement threshold. Lower-value opportunities go to Contracts Finder. Older legacy notices may still appear in OJEU/Find-a-Tender style; current procurements use Procurement Act notices.

contracts-finder.name: Contracts Finder
contracts-finder.intro: Contracts Finder lists lower-value central government and NHS opportunities, plus pipelines and award notices. Same model as FTS: a notice service that points you at the buyer's actual e-sourcing platform. Threshold is generally over £12,000 including VAT for central government and over £30,000 including VAT for sub-central bodies including NHS trusts.
hoops: A free account lets you save searches and get email alerts. You do not need one to browse.
You will still need to register on whatever portal the notice links to (Atamis, ProContract, Jaggaer, In-tend, etc) to actually bid.
steps: Set up a Contracts Finder account and save searches on CPV codes plus 'NHS', 'digital', 'AI', 'analytics', 'patient record'.
When a relevant notice lands, read the contract value and notice type carefully. A 'Pipeline' notice is early warning, not a live tender.
Click the buyer's link, register on their portal, download the documents.
Mark the deadline in your calendar and work back: SQ first, then ITT, then clarifications window.
contracts-finder.gotchas: Some NHS trusts under-publish to Contracts Finder and only post on their own e-sourcing system. The MyClinical Growth daily brief picks up both.

atamis.name: Atamis (Health Family eCommercial System)
atamis.intro: Atamis is the NHS Health Family eCommercial system used by many NHS organisations and NHS England for procurement activity, tenders, contracts, supplier management and related commercial workflows. NHS England has previously reported tens of thousands of registered suppliers. NOE CPC has also adopted Atamis, so its coverage is expanding beyond the original Health Family.
hoops: Free supplier registration is required to see most opportunity detail and to bid.
Registration asks for company information, financial details, certifications, and a self-assessment of categories you supply.
Pick category codes carefully. Alerts run off them. Tick too few and you miss opportunities; tick everything and the inbox is unreadable.
steps: Register a supplier account at the Atamis Health Family supplier portal.
Complete your supplier profile fully. Buyers see this when shortlisting.
Set category alerts. For digital health these usually sit under IT services, clinical software, medical devices and consultancy.
When a notice lands, express interest in the system to receive the SQ/ITT documents.
Use the in-portal clarifications tab. Questions asked there are answered to all bidders, which can shape your response.
atamis.gotchas: Atamis category coverage is uneven across organisations. A digital health tender at one trust may sit under 'IT consultancy', at another under 'Clinical systems'. Set multiple alerts.

sbs.name: NHS Shared Business Services (SBS)
sbs.intro: NHS SBS is a DHSC / Sopra Steria joint venture that runs national framework agreements for NHS and public sector organisations. They are the operator of the £900m (including VAT) Healthcare AI Solutions framework. SBS is the source most likely to release a major framework with almost no public noise. The 'stealth release' problem.
hoops: Submissions for SBS frameworks go through the e-sourcing platform named in each notice. The Healthcare AI Solutions framework, for example, uses SAP Ariba.
Register on the relevant platform with company details, references and category interests.

For most clinical or data frameworks expect to need: ISO 27001 (or a credible roadmap), Cyber Essentials or Cyber Essentials Plus, DCB0129 clinical safety case where the product affects clinical decisions, DTAC self-assessment, GDPR/DPIA documentation. Exact requirements depend on the framework and lot.
Framework competitions usually have a formal Selection Questionnaire (SQ) gate before you reach the technical response. Fail the SQ and you do not see the ITT.
steps: Subscribe to the SBS framework news feed and the MyClinical Growth daily brief so you do not miss a launch.
When a framework opens, identify the named e-sourcing platform from the FTS notice and register there.
Download the FTS notice plus the SBS-specific framework documents. The lot structure is often the most important thing to read first.
Decide which lots you are realistically credible in. Bidding for too many dilutes the response and looks unfocused.
Build the SQ response against the actual scoring criteria, not against what you wish was being asked. Then write the ITT.
Submit at least 24 hours before the deadline. Portal upload failures in the final hour are common.
sbs.gotchas: SBS frameworks often run for four years. Missing one means a four-year wait. Clinical safety and DTAC paperwork takes weeks, not days, so start it the moment you see a planned procurement notice (not the day the ITT drops).

nhs-supply-chain.name: NHS Supply Chain (Medical Technology Innovation DPS)
nhs-supply-chain.intro: NHS Supply Chain (Supply Chain Coordination Ltd) is the main route for products and consumables into the NHS. For innovation, the relevant route is the Medical Technology Innovation DPS (a seven-year dynamic purchasing system, often referred to in market shorthand as 'Spark' or 'Innovation DPS'). Unlike a framework, the DPS is open to new suppliers continuously, not just at refresh windows.
hoops: Free registration on the Supply Chain supplier portal.
Innovation DPS application asks for product description, evidence (clinical, regulatory, real-world), and a structured questionnaire response.
Where the product is a regulated medical device, CE/UKCA marking is required, and for software-as-medical-device that includes a current technical file.
steps: Identify which Innovation DPS category your product fits. Categories are narrow on purpose. The wrong category will get you rejected on a technicality.
Apply via the Innovation DPS portal. Turnaround is usually weeks rather than months.
Once accepted, you sit on the DPS and respond to mini-competitions when NHS organisations call off.
Keep your DPS profile up to date. Buyers filter on it.
nhs-supply-chain.gotchas: Strongest for medtech, devices, diagnostics and connected hardware. Pure software (e.g. SaaS clinical decision support) is a weaker fit and is better served by G-Cloud or the NHS Innovation Service.

ccs-health.name: Crown Commercial Service (CCS), Health
ccs-health.intro: CCS runs cross-government commercial agreements used by NHS organisations, including G-Cloud and several technology, digital, cyber and consultancy frameworks. To bid you go via the framework you care about, not via CCS as a single portal. CCS does not run Spark / Innovation DPS, which sits with NHS Supply Chain.
hoops: Each CCS framework has its own qualification rules and its own bid window. Frameworks refresh on different cycles.
Expect Cyber Essentials as a baseline. Cyber Essentials Plus or ISO 27001 where risk, data sensitivity or framework rules require it.
Financial standing tests are typical. Two years of audited accounts and a turnover threshold relative to the contract value.
steps: Use the CCS pipeline page to see which frameworks are open, in market, or coming.
Pick the one or two you are realistic for. Resist the urge to apply to everything.
Watch the planned-procurement notice on FTS for the precise SQ/ITT release date.
Apply within the window. Out-of-window applications cannot be considered (not negotiable).
ccs-health.gotchas: Frameworks have hard application windows. Miss them and you wait until the next refresh, which can be two to four years away.

g-cloud.name: G-Cloud / Digital Marketplace
g-cloud.intro: G-Cloud is the main route for cloud hosting, SaaS and cloud support into the public sector, including the NHS. If you sell software-as-a-service to clinicians or administrators, being on G-Cloud is the difference between being findable and not.
hoops: Application windows open roughly annually. You can only apply when CCS opens a new iteration (G-Cloud 14, 15, etc).
Cyber Essentials is the baseline and Cyber Essentials Plus is commonly required, depending on the iteration and service type.
You publish a service description, pricing card and terms. These are public and searchable. Buyers compare on them.
Where consultancy or implementation services are included, buyers often expect clear role-based day rates, commonly mapped to SFIA-style levels.
steps: Watch for the next G-Cloud application window opening on the Digital Marketplace site or in the daily brief.
Prepare your service descriptions in advance. Each service is a separate listing and needs its own description and pricing.
Submit during the window. Late submissions are not accepted.
Once listed, NHS buyers find you via search. The way you write your service title and description determines whether you appear.
g-cloud.gotchas: Being on G-Cloud does not generate sales by itself. NHS buyers still need to find you and run a call-off mini-competition. Treat your G-Cloud listing as SEO: keywords matter.

hssf.name: Health Systems Support Framework (HSSF)
hssf.intro: HSSF is NHS England's framework for population health management, advanced analytics and digital and service transformation suppliers. NHS England has stopped initiating new call-offs and existing ones continue. Do not treat HSSF as an active route for new suppliers.
hoops: If you are already on it, existing call-offs continue.
New suppliers cannot currently get on. Watch for successor or replacement routes.
steps: Track NHS England digital transformation pipeline announcements.
Subscribe to the daily brief so you see any successor framework launch the day it goes live.
hssf.gotchas: Energy is better spent on G-Cloud, SBS frameworks, or NHS Innovation Service rather than chasing HSSF.

nhs-england-procurement.name: NHS England Procurement & Commercial
nhs-england-procurement.intro: NHS England's procurement team covers the full lifecycle: market engagement, supplier selection, contract management and expiry planning. Central programmes (e.g. the Federated Data Platform, national digital programmes) tend to be very large, very visible, and go via Atamis or directly via Find a Tender.
hoops: For digital, data or clinical programmes, expect requirements around ISO 27001, Cyber Essentials (often Plus), clinical safety (DCB0129/0160), data protection, DTAC or equivalent assurance, NHS Data Security and Protection Toolkit submission and DPIA materials. Exact mix depends on the programme.
Past performance with the NHS is heavily weighted. First-time NHS suppliers should expect to partner with an established prime.
steps: Track the NHS England procurement pipeline page and FTS notices labelled 'NHS England'.
For large programmes, decide early whether you are bidding as prime or sub. Then build the consortium accordingly.
Engage in the pre-market engagement events. They are not just for show. The questions you ask shape the final specification.
nhs-england-procurement.gotchas: Not SME-friendly procurements. If you are pre-revenue, the realistic route is to partner with a prime rather than bid as lead.

noe-cpc.name: NOE CPC (North of England Commercial Procurement Collaborative)
aliases: NOE CPC (NHS North of England Commercial Procurement Collaborative)
NHS North of England Commercial Procurement Collaborative
noe-cpc.intro: NOE CPC is an NHS England Central Commercial Function accredited framework host that runs frameworks for trusts in the North of England (and beyond). They run several digital and clinical frameworks each year.
hoops: NOE CPC has moved into the Atamis Health Family environment, so do not assume In-tend or any other platform unless the live notice says so.
Free supplier registration.
Standard NHS supplier requirements apply: insurance, financial standing, relevant certifications for the category.
steps: Register on Atamis if you have not already.
Set category alerts for NOE CPC's framework families.
When a notice lands, follow the named platform in the notice. Express interest there to receive documents.
Submit within deadline. Portal-only submission rules are strict.
noe-cpc.gotchas: NOE CPC publishes a yearly pipeline. Read it in January. Many launches happen Q2 and Q3.

lpp.name: NHS London Procurement Partnership (LPP)
lpp.intro: LPP is one of the NHS procurement hubs, serving London and beyond. Digital and clinical frameworks come up regularly. Bidding goes via the e-sourcing platform LPP names in each notice.
hoops: Free supplier registration on the relevant LPP portal.
Standard SQ process.
steps: Register on the named LPP portal.
Set alerts on the categories that match.
Express interest in tenders you want to receive.
lpp.gotchas: London reference sites and mature clinical safety documentation are commercially helpful, especially for digital health suppliers. Have your DCB0129 evidence ready to cite, not assemble.

ee-collab.name: East of England NHS Collaborative Procurement Hub
aliases: East of England NHS Collaborative Procurement Hub
ee-collab.intro: Regional collaborative procurement hub. Runs frameworks and call-offs for East of England trusts. The hub now links suppliers to Atamis as the single eCommercial system used across the NHS, alongside its own e-tendering portal.
hoops: Always follow the portal named in the live notice.
steps: Monitor the hub's tender pipeline and Atamis-linked opportunities.
Register on whichever portal the live notice points to.
ee-collab.gotchas: Smaller hub, less predictable cadence. Worth setting an alert on Atamis under 'East of England' rather than checking the hub site directly.

nhs-commercial-solutions.name: NHS Commercial Solutions
nhs-commercial-solutions.intro: NHS Commercial Solutions is the South East NHS procurement hub and one of the four NHS Procurement in Partnership hubs alongside NOE CPC, LPP and East of England. It runs collaborative frameworks across clinical and corporate categories.
hoops: Bid via the e-sourcing portal the notice points to.
Standard NHS supplier requirements apply.
steps: Register on the relevant portal.
Express interest in tenders that match.
nhs-commercial-solutions.gotchas: Coverage overlaps with LPP for some London-fringe trusts. The notice itself will tell you which hub is running it.

healthtrust-europe.name: HealthTrust Europe
healthtrust-europe.intro: HealthTrust Europe is a procurement partner to over a thousand organisations across public and private sectors. Its public sector frameworks are designed for contracting authorities, particularly those delivering health and social care. Less common for pure digital health, more common for medtech, devices and outsourced clinical services. Worth knowing about if your product crosses into medtech.
hoops: Supplier registration on the HTE supplier portal.
Strong commercial terms expected. HTE is a buying group, not a charity.
steps: Register if your product fits one of the HTE categories.
Engage early with the commercial team where appropriate. The commercial model and framework access route can differ from NHS-owned procurement hubs, and the unwritten rules are real.
healthtrust-europe.gotchas: Some trusts use HTE in parallel with NHS Supply Chain. Check whether the buyer is HTE-affiliated before pitching commercials.

pcs.name: Public Contracts Scotland (PCS)
pcs.intro: PCS is Scotland's official public sector opportunity portal, used for Scottish public sector notices including NHS Scotland. PCS-Tender is the linked national e-tendering system.
hoops: Free registration on PCS gives you alerts.
PCS-Tender (separate) is where you actually submit bids.
steps: Register on PCS, set CPV and keyword alerts.
When a notice lands, follow through to PCS-Tender and register a bid account.
Submit before deadline. Late submissions are rejected automatically by the system.
pcs.gotchas: Do not assume English DTAC alone satisfies Scottish requirements. Check NHS Scotland information governance, cyber, clinical safety and technical assurance requirements for the specific procurement.

sell2wales.name: Sell2Wales
sell2wales.intro: Welsh Government-backed public procurement portal. NHS Wales (via NHS Wales Shared Services Partnership, NWSSP) advertises tender opportunities here.
hoops: Free registration for alerts.
Bids go via eTenderWales or the platform the notice specifies.
steps: Register on Sell2Wales.
Set alerts on NHS Wales and health-related CPV codes.
Follow through to the named platform to bid.
sell2wales.gotchas: Welsh-language considerations apply for patient-facing services. Check the specification.

etendersni.name: eTendersNI
etendersni.intro: eTendersNI is the e-tendering platform for Northern Ireland public sector opportunities, including HSC procurements advertised by BSO PaLS.
hoops: Free registration.
NI HSC has a distinct procurement architecture. Trusts buy via BSO PaLS for most goods and services.
steps: Register on eTendersNI.
Set alerts for HSC NI categories.
etendersni.gotchas: Lower volume than England, Scotland or Wales. Worth tracking if you want UK-wide coverage.

nhs-innovation-service.name: NHS Innovation Service
nhs-innovation-service.intro: Not a tender portal. A national front door for innovators to get free, structured support from NHS England, the Health Innovation Networks, NICE and others. The right starting point if you are pre-procurement and want the NHS to take you seriously.
hoops: Free to join.
You submit your innovation through a structured form. NHS Innovation Service assigns you support based on your maturity (idea, evidence-gathering, scaling).
Treat it as a readiness and navigation route, not a direct sales channel. Editorially, expect a 6 to 12 month engagement before it pays off.
steps: Register and submit your innovation at the NHS Innovation Service site.
Be honest about your evidence base. Overclaiming gets you assigned to the wrong support tier.
Engage with the assigned advisors. The introductions they make are the actual value.
nhs-innovation-service.gotchas: Do not expect contracts to drop out of this. Expect a clearer evidence pathway, the right people in your network, and a stronger story by the time you go to procurement.

nice-htap.name: NICE digital health technology pathways
nice-htap.intro: From April 2026 NICE expanded its technology appraisal routes to cover selected digital health technologies, creating a national reimbursement pathway where positive guidance applies. This puts high-impact healthtech that meets critical NHS needs on a footing closer to medicines for reimbursement, but only for selected, high-impact tools, not every digital product.
hoops: Eligible products are typically digital health technologies and medtech with a credible cost-saving or outcomes case.
You submit an evidence package against the NICE Evidence Standards Framework (ESF). Use the ESF to understand evidence expectations rather than treating it as a tick-box.
Expect months, not weeks. This is the real route to system-wide adoption.
steps: Read the NICE ESF carefully and self-assess your evidence tier honestly.
If your evidence justifies it, apply via the NICE digital health team.
Build the evidence dossier they ask for. Independent evaluation is heavily weighted.
nice-htap.gotchas: If your evidence is mostly developer claims with no comparative data, invest in a real-world evaluation before applying. The committee will not approve on assertions.

hin.name: Health Innovation Networks (15 regional)
hin.intro: 15 regional Health Innovation Networks (formerly Academic Health Science Networks), established by NHS England to spread innovation at pace and scale. Free support, real networks, occasional small contracts. HINs open doors, support evidence generation and help adoption. They do not replace procurement.
hoops: Free to engage. You pitch your innovation to the regional HIN.
Each HIN has its own focus areas. Check before you approach.
steps: Identify the HIN(s) whose region and priorities match your product.
Use their innovator support form to introduce yourself.
Ask for introductions to specific trusts, ICBs or clinical leads. That is the real value.
hin.gotchas: HINs are not procurement bodies. They open doors. The actual contract still goes through a normal NHS procurement route.

aac.name: Accelerated Access Collaborative (AAC)
aac.intro: AAC sits inside NHS England as a national innovation adoption layer, not a procurement portal. Relevant levers include the NHS Innovation Accelerator (delivered with the 15 Health Innovation Networks), Rapid Uptake Products, the MedTech Funding Mandate, and the Innovation for Healthcare Inequalities Programme (InHIP).
hoops: AAC programmes are application-based and competitive. They want evidence, not pitches.
Most AAC routes expect NICE guidance or equivalent independent endorsement of cost-effectiveness.
steps: Identify the right AAC programme for your product (Innovation Accelerator if you are scaling, InHIP if your product addresses health inequalities, MTFM if NICE has approved you).
Apply during the open window. Windows are usually annual.
Lean on your HIN to support the application.
aac.gotchas: AAC programmes are signalling, not direct procurement. Selection means NHS England visibly endorses you, which makes ICB conversations easier.

gp-it-futures.name: Digital Care Services / Digital Services for Integrated Care
aliases: Tech Innovation Framework / GP IT Futures (Digital Care Services)
gp-it-futures.intro: Digital Care Services and Digital Services for Integrated Care are the current catalogue and assurance route for GP and primary-care digital services. GP IT Futures and the Tech Innovation Framework sit within that broader evolution. If you sell anything that touches primary care, this is the gating step.
hoops: Assurance against the NHS England Digital Care Services Capabilities and Standards. Detailed and prescriptive.
Expect DTAC, DCB0129, GDPR/DPIA, and NHS Data Security and Protection Toolkit submission.
Catalogue approval is staged. Expect months.
steps: Read the relevant catalogue capability specification.
Self-assess against it honestly. Gaps mean rejection.
Apply via the Digital Care Services route. Buyer mini-competitions follow.
Once on, GP practices and PCNs can call you off the catalogue.
gp-it-futures.gotchas: The platform has been evolving from GP IT Futures into a broader integrated care model. If primary care is a target market, treat catalogue presence as table-stakes.

mtfm.name: MedTech Funding Mandate (MTFM)
mtfm.intro: MTFM creates a national policy expectation and funding support for selected NICE-recommended cost-saving technologies, making local adoption much easier than normal procurement routes. The strongest commercial signal in the system for the products it covers.
hoops: You need positive NICE guidance (Medical Technologies, Diagnostics, or relevant HealthTech route) with a cost-saving conclusion.
The product must be ready to scale, not a pilot.
steps: Get NICE evaluation first. Without it, MTFM is not available to you.
Once approved by NICE on cost-saving terms, AAC notifies you of eligibility for MTFM listing.
Listing brings active commissioner engagement and ICB rollout support.
mtfm.gotchas: Only a small number of products carry MTFM at any time. Getting on is a multi-year journey. Plan investment accordingly.

ccs-tech.name: CCS technology & digital frameworks (beyond G-Cloud)
ccs-tech.intro: CCS runs wider technology, cyber, software, data and digital services frameworks beyond G-Cloud. These can be relevant for healthtech suppliers providing implementation, analytics, cyber, integration or specialist delivery services around the core product. Specific framework names change, expire and refresh, so check the CCS pipeline for the current set.
hoops: Each framework has its own application window and SQ rules.
Expect Cyber Essentials as a baseline, and Cyber Essentials Plus or ISO 27001 where risk, data sensitivity or framework rules require it.
Sub-contractor disclosure is required. Plan your supply chain early.
steps: Use the CCS pipeline page to plan your applications 6-12 months ahead.
Apply within the window for each framework that matches.
Once on, monitor the framework's call-off competitions for NHS buyers.
ccs-tech.gotchas: Application windows close. Set calendar reminders before each framework's expected refresh date. The cost of missing one is 2-4 years of exclusion.

trust-esourcing.name: Trust / ICB e-sourcing platforms
aliases: Trust / ICB e-sourcing platforms (ProContract, In-tend, Delta eSourcing, Jaggaer)
trust-esourcing.intro: Individual trusts, ICBs and CSUs may still use local e-sourcing platforms such as ProContract, In-tend, Delta eSourcing, Jaggaer or Atamis. When you click through from a Contracts Finder or FTS notice, the public notice should tell you which platform to express interest and submit on.
hoops: Each platform needs its own free supplier account.
Platforms are buyer-agnostic. The same login lets you bid on every organisation that uses that platform.
steps: Create accounts on at least: Atamis, ProContract, In-tend, Delta eSourcing and Jaggaer. Free, takes 10 minutes each.
Set alerts where the platform allows it.
Always express interest in a notice via the buyer's chosen platform. That is the only way to receive the documents.
trust-esourcing.gotchas: Some trusts run procurements only via their local platform, never via Atamis or Find a Tender. The MyClinical Growth daily brief catches what it can, but trust portals are the long tail of NHS procurement.

devolved.name: Devolved NHS national procurement bodies (NSS, NWSSP, BSO PaLS)
aliases: Devolved NHS national procurement bodies (NSS, NWSSP, BSO PaLS)
devolved.intro: NHS Scotland (NSS National Procurement), NHS Wales (NWSSP) and Health and Social Care NI (BSO PaLS) each run their own national procurement bodies. Same idea as NHS Supply Chain in England but with different rules, portals and assurance regimes.
hoops: Each devolved nation has its own supplier registration.
Scotland: NSS uses PCS for tender notices and has its own information governance and cyber requirements.
Wales: NWSSP uses Sell2Wales and its e-tendering portal.
NI: BSO PaLS uses eTendersNI.
steps: Register on each devolved body's supplier portal if you intend to sell into that nation.
Map your English certifications to their equivalents. Some translate, some do not.
Plan for separate procurement cycles. The devolved nations do not follow NHS England's calendar.
devolved.gotchas: DTAC is England-specific. Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland may recognise some of the same underlying evidence, but suppliers should check local IG, cyber, clinical safety and technical assurance requirements before claiming compliance.
